claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First of all, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, eligibility for Child Benefit depends on their current situation. Child Benefit usually stops when a child turns 16, but can continue until they're 20 if they're in approved education or training.

Could you tell me, is Alex currently in full-time education or on an approved training course? If so, could you provide some details about what they're studying or training in?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form college. That's helpful information.

I have a couple more questions to help determine your eligibility:

1. First, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's great to know.

Just a couple more questions:

Are you responsible for Alex in terms of paying for things like food, clothes, and accommodation? In other words, are you financially supporting him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I pay for his food, clothes, and all his living expenses since he lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Do you live in the UK, and are you allowed to be in the UK (i.e., there are no immigration restrictions on your stay)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, I'm allowed to be here, no immigration issues.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I now have everything I need to make a decision about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You live in the UK (Manchester) with no immigration restrictions
- Alex is 18 years old and lives with you
- Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college)
- You are responsible for Alex and pay for his food, clothes, and living expenses

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 18):**
- ✅ Although Alex is over 16, Child Benefit can continue until age 20 as long as the child stays in approved education or training
- ✅ Studying A-levels at sixth form college counts as approved education
- ✅ Alex lives with you and you are responsible for him (paying for food, clothes, and living expenses)
- ✅ You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions

**Important things to note:**
- Child Benefit will continue for Alex as long as he remains in approved education or training, up until he turns 20
- If Alex finishes his A-levels and doesn't continue in approved education, or if he starts working 24 hours or more per week, you'll need to inform the Child Benefit Office as your eligibility may change

If you haven't already, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which is above the standard Child Benefit age of 16. However, the rules state that you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education. Additionally, Alex lives with his parent who is responsible for him and pays for his food, clothes, and living expenses. The claimant lives in the UK and has no immigration restrictions. All eligibility criteria are therefore met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with no immigration issues and is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 18), who lives with them. Although Alex is over 16, he remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college), which means Child Benefit can continue until he turns 20, as long as he stays in approved education or training. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
